# Distributional-output/adequacy (DG) axis -- hand-authored summary

**This file is hand-authored, not generated by `tools/capability_ledger.py`.**
It follows the same "code admission vs. validation evidence" spirit as the
generated `docs/dev-log/dashboard/capability-surface.md`, but is a separate,
manually maintained note rather than a third ledger axis. See "Why not a
generator axis" below for the reason integration was deferred.

Scope: the distributional-output/adequacy layer (#747 adequacy diagnostics,
#748 distributional outputs) built on `fitted_distribution()`/
`drm_family_dpq()` (`R/family-dpq.R`). Two evidence gates:

- **DG2** -- per-family `{d,p,q}` correctness: `p(q(u)) == u` inverse
  identity, normalization at the support boundary, agreement with the
  compiled log-density at the fitted `theta_hat`, and an EXTERNAL reference
  (base-R `d/p/qFAMILY()`, or a hand-built atom/mixture decomposition where
  no external function exists). Blocking before a family is promoted to
  `status = "reference"`.
- **DG3** -- behavioural recovery: the diagnostic controls type-I error
  under a correctly specified fit and has quantified power against named
  mis-specifications. Fixed-effect only; plug-in, not calibrated coverage.

## 18/18 families at DG-reference

Every fitted `model_type` is promoted to `status = "reference"` in
`R/family-dpq.R`: 17 `model_type`s set it directly in their own
`drm_family_dpq_*()` closure, and `biv_gaussian`'s closure
(`drm_family_dpq_biv_gaussian()`) reuses `drm_family_dpq_gaussian()`
verbatim, inheriting the same status -- 18 `model_type`s total:
`gaussian`, `biv_gaussian`, `student`, `lognormal`, `gamma`, `poisson`,
`nbinom2`, `zi_poisson`, `zi_nbinom2`, `beta`, `truncated_nbinom2`,
`hurdle_nbinom2`, `cumulative_logit`, `beta_binomial`, `zero_one_beta`,
`tweedie`, `skew_normal`, `binomial` -- the same 18-route set the
missing-response axis uses (`ROUTES` in `tools/capability_ledger.py`).

Two firewall notes, both tested (`tests/testthat/test-dg-firewall.R`):

- `skew_normal`'s DG2/DG3 promotion is a distributional-output-axis result
  only. It does not certify `skew_normal`'s own fit-quality status
  (`diagnostic_hold` in `check_drm()`), which is a separate axis.
- `biv_gaussian` is **marginal-only**: its `{d,p,q}` describe one response's
  `N(mu_k, sigma_k)` marginal, independent of `rho12`, never the joint
  bivariate distribution.

DG2 evidence: `tests/testthat/test-family-dpq*.R` (batches A-D, one file per
family group). DG3 local smoke evidence: the same files' "DG3 smoke" tests
(fixed-seed, n in the low hundreds, CRAN-lane fast). DG3 gated evidence: the
400-seed campaign below (NOT_CRAN, run on Totoro/DRAC per project doctrine).

## Gated DG3 power-arm campaign (400 seeds/arm, alpha = 0.05; tweedie 99)

Source: `docs/dev-log/simulation-artifacts/2026-07-12-dg3-power-arm-gated/summary-gated-full.tsv`.
Arm "A" (or the first `role = true` row) is the type-I control arm: the
diagnostic applied to a correctly specified fit. Each named arm's `wrong`
row is power: the diagnostic applied to a fit that omits the named
mechanism. Arms marked `*` are declared substitute arms (the originally
designed mis-spec was infeasible to simulate cleanly for that family; the
substitute is usually a mean-structure mis-spec, so its lower power is
expected, not a distributional blind spot). `tweedie` is the one family run
at a reduced 99 seeds/arm (`run-tweedie-capped.R`/`tweedie-capped.out` in
the source directory), a compute-cost cap on the compound Poisson-gamma
simulator, not a hidden dropped-seed adjustment; every other family used
the full 400 seeds/arm.

| Family | Type-I (arm A) | Mis-spec arm | Power (n=300-400) |
|---|---:|---|---:|
| `gaussian` | 0.0075 | heavy-tailed wrong family | 0.925 |
| | | heteroscedastic location-only | 1.000 |
| `biv_gaussian` | 0.0075 | heteroscedastic location-only (response 1) | 1.000 |
| | | omitted covariate (response 1)* | 0.0125 |
| `student` | 0.005 | Gaussian wrong family (heavy tail) | 0.995 |
| | | heteroscedastic location-only (absorbed by `nu`) | 0.035 |
| `lognormal` | 0.0075 | dispersion varying, mean-only fit | 0.8425 |
| | | wrong family: gamma on lognormal data | 0.1875 |
| `gamma` | 0.0125 | dispersion varying, mean-only fit | 1.000 |
| | | wrong family: gamma on lognormal data | 0.1875 |
| `poisson` | 0.0225 | excess zeros ignored | 0.9625 |
| | | overdispersion, wrong family | 0.9825 |
| `nbinom2` | 0.0075 | excess zeros ignored (absorbed by `sigma`) | 0.035 |
| | | Poisson ignoring overdispersion | 0.9825 |
| `zi_poisson` | 0.0125 | ZI ignored, fit plain Poisson | 0.9625 |
| | | ZI mechanism misset (constant vs. x) | 0.02 |
| `zi_nbinom2` | 0.000 | ZI ignored, fit plain NB2 | 0.035 |
| | | ZI mechanism misset (constant vs. x) | 0.04 |
| `beta` | 0.005 | dispersion varying, mean-only fit | 0.8127 |
| | | omitted nonlinear mean* | 0.2775 |
| `truncated_nbinom2` | 0.005 | dispersion varying, mean-only fit* | 0.01 |
| | | truncation ignored, fit plain NB2 | 1.000 |
| `hurdle_nbinom2` | 0.005 | hurdle mechanism misset (constant vs. x) | 0.01 |
| | | hurdle ignored, fit plain NB2 | 0.0125 |
| `cumulative_logit` | 0.005 | non-proportional odds | 0.085 |
| | | omitted covariate* | 0.0075 |
| `beta_binomial` | 0.0175 | binomial ignoring overdispersion | 1.000 |
| | | dispersion varying, mean-only fit* | 0.0475 |
| `zero_one_beta` | 0.005 | atoms ignored, fit plain beta* | 1.000 |
| | | ZOI mechanism misset (constant vs. x) | 0.000 |
| `tweedie` | 0.0101 | dispersion varying, mean-only fit* | 0.1429 |
| | | atom ignored, fit plain gamma* | 0.9899 |
| `skew_normal` | 0.0075 | heteroscedastic location-only | 0.9675 |
| | | wrong family: Gaussian ignoring skew | 0.9775 |
| `binomial` | 0.0125 | omitted covariate* | 1.000 |
| | | overdispersion, wrong family (beta-binomial) | 1.000 |

Type-I error is at or below the nominal 0.05 rate in every arm-A cell above
(range 0-0.0225) -- the underlying KS+PIT statistic is conservative, so
reported power is understated, not overstated. Within the capped
`tweedie` run, the `dispersion_varying_mean_only_SUBSTITUTE` arm additionally
lost 66/99 (`true`) and 22/99 (`wrong`) seeds to non-convergence -- tracked
explicitly in `n_fail`/`n_ok` in the source `.tsv`, not silently dropped --
so its 0.1429 power figure rests on a smaller effective seed count (77) than
the other tweedie arms (99).

### n-ladder arms (mechanism mis-specification, checked for a sample-size fix)

Four families have a "constant-vs-covariate" inflation/hurdle/atom
*mechanism* mis-spec that stayed near the type-I rate at n = 300-400; the
n-ladder checks whether more data rescues detection.

| Family (n-ladder) | n | Arm | Type-I (true) | Power (wrong) |
|---|---:|---|---:|---:|
| `hurdle_nbinom2` (hu-mechanism) | 1000 | hurdle mechanism misset | 0.02 | 0.005 |
| `hurdle_nbinom2` (hu-mechanism) | 3000 | hurdle mechanism misset | 0.025 | 0.005 |
| `zero_one_beta` (zoi-mechanism) | 1000 | ZOI mechanism misset | 0.005 | 0.0025 |
| `zero_one_beta` (zoi-mechanism) | 3000 | ZOI mechanism misset | 0.000 | 0.000 |
| `zi_nbinom2` (zi-mechanism) | 1000 | ZI mechanism misset | 0.0125 | 0.0275 |
| `zi_nbinom2` (zi-mechanism) | 3000 | ZI mechanism misset | 0.02 | 0.1075 |
| `zi_poisson` (zi-mechanism) | 1000 | ZI mechanism misset | 0.0225 | 0.0325 |
| `zi_poisson` (zi-mechanism) | 3000 | ZI mechanism misset | 0.0175 | 0.06 |

Also n-laddered: `gamma`-vs-`lognormal` wrong-family detection is
sample-size-**limited**, not structurally blind -- power rises from
0.1875-0.7925 at n=300-1000 to 1.000 at n=3000 (`lognormal_gammawrong`
rows above).

**Reading the mechanism-arm table.** Unlike the heteroscedasticity/
overdispersion arms above (which reach power >= 0.92 at n=300), all four
mechanism arms stay well under 0.8 even at n = 3000 -- but they split into
two genuinely different patterns, not one.

- `hurdle_nbinom2` and `zero_one_beta`: power is FLAT and near the type-I
  rate at every n (`hurdle_nbinom2`: 0.01, 0.005, 0.005 at n=300/1000/3000;
  `zero_one_beta`: 0.000, 0.0025, 0.000). This is the diagnostic's
  structural blind spot, not a sample-size problem: a constant
  inflation/hurdle probability and a covariate-varying one produce the same
  MARGINAL distribution of quantile residuals once the model's own free
  inflation parameter has absorbed the average level, so no amount of
  additional data closes the gap with this diagnostic.
- `zi_poisson` and `zi_nbinom2`: power RISES with n (`zi_poisson`: 0.02,
  0.0325, 0.06 at n=300/1000/3000; `zi_nbinom2`: 0.04, 0.0275, 0.1075) --
  their marginals do differ under the mechanism mis-spec, so this is not a
  structural blind spot. But power remains far below the >= 0.8 detectable
  benchmark even at n=3000, so the mis-specification is sample-size-limited
  in principle but impractical to detect at realistic sample sizes with
  this diagnostic.

## The structural blind spot, stated once

A mis-specification that a fitted family's own free nuisance/dispersion/
inflation parameter can absorb leaves the fitted-model's quantile residual
marginally close to N(0,1) and is **not detectable** by this diagnostic --
see the low-power rows above (`nbinom2` excess-zeros 0.035, `student`
heteroscedasticity 0.035, and the two flat mechanism arms `hurdle_nbinom2`/
`zero_one_beta`, <= 0.01 even at n=3000). `zi_poisson`/`zi_nbinom2`'s
mechanism arms are a related but distinct case: power rises with n (to
0.06/0.11 at n=3000) rather than staying flat, so they are
sample-size-limited in principle rather than structurally blind, but they
stay far below 0.8 at every n tested, so they remain undetectable in
practice at realistic sample sizes. A mean-structure diagnostic, not this
one, is what catches the genuinely structural cases. This is documented in
full prose in `docs/dev-log/known-limitations.md` (the
"Distributional-adequacy diagnostic scope" bullet) and in the roxygen
Details of `residuals.drmTMB()` (`R/adequacy.R`) and `worm_plot()`/
`qq_plot()` (`R/adequacy-plots.R`).

## Firewall

A DG2/DG3 tick never promotes, or otherwise changes, a family's separate
inference-tier status (Wald/profile/bootstrap interval maturity in
`docs/dev-log/known-limitations.md` / `vignettes/capability-and-limits.Rmd`).
Enforced by `tests/testthat/test-dg-firewall.R`: exercising
`fitted_distribution()` and `residuals(type = "quantile")` on a
`skew_normal` fit (whose `check_drm()` status is `diagnostic_hold`), and
additionally `predict(type = "quantile")` and `exceedance()` on a `gaussian`
fit, leaves `check_drm()`'s output byte-identical before and after in both
cases.

## Why not a generator axis (deferred)

`tools/capability_ledger.py`'s existing `missing_response` axis is not a
generic "second axis" mechanism -- it is a purpose-built ledger with
axis-specific business rules baked into `validate()` (lines ~447-508):
hardcoded `test_gate` semantics (G0-G5 mapped to specific
admission/rejection/masking/recovery evidence classes that only make sense
for missing-response routes), a hardcoded `expected_counts` dict in
`schema.json`, and generator functions (`missing_markdown()`,
`surface_markdown()`, `surface_html()`, the HTML/CSS route cards) written
against that one axis's fields. A DG axis has different gate semantics
(DG2/DG3, not G0-G5; type-I + named-arm power, not
admission/masking/recovery), so adding it correctly is a multi-file
change -- new rows in `capability-ledger/{cells,evidence,transitions}.tsv`,
an axis-aware branch in `validate()`'s gate-consistency checks, a new
`schema.json` axis entry + `expected_counts`, and a new markdown/HTML
section -- not a focused ~1-file change to `capability_ledger.py` alone.
Per the landing-slice brief, that integration is deferred; this
hand-authored file is the interim record. A follow-up ticket should either
(a) generalize `validate()`'s gate-consistency block to be axis-parameterized
before adding a third axis, or (b) keep DG as a permanently
separately-maintained note like this one, mirroring how
`docs/design/168-r-julia-finish-capability-matrix.md` sits outside the
ledger today.

## Does not cover

This note does not establish DG4/DG5 calibrated coverage, random-effect or
structured residual adequacy, bivariate joint (non-marginal) outputs, or any
change to a family's own inference-tier status. See the ultra-plan's "Not
done" list (`docs/dev-log/2026-07-12-distributional-output-adequacy-layer-ultra-plan.md`).
